scope: norway-vs-france and senegal-vs-iraq third-round tactical coaching packet

motivation_summary.summary: draw should preserve group-first position under standard goal-difference logic; already qualified
must push for win if group-first objective is prioritized; already qualified
win-first script with likely goal-difference awareness; low value in conservative draw management
win-first script but likely from low-block base because forward availability and ceiling remain constrained

data_gaps: final 24h recovery note for rotation candidates unavailable

uncertain_slots: central defense partner may rotate with other senior option
one attacking slot could be sacrificed for extra midfielder if Deschamps prioritizes control
predicted_substitution_triggers: protect lead or draw with extra midfielder
add pure wing speed only if chasing or if Norway fullbacks tire

uncertain_slots: Oscar Bobb is a real alternative if Solbakken wants more dribble creation
predicted_substitution_triggers: introduce Oscar Bobb if trailing and chance creation stalls
protect box with extra height only if Norway surprisingly leads late

key_matchups.battle: Odegaard right-half-space distribution vs France holding-screen and left-centre coverage
key_matchups.impact: determines whether Norway can connect Haaland and Sorloth with clean first-phase service

key_matchups.battle: Mbappe plus Theo acceleration vs Norway right-side recovery chain
key_matchups.impact: France's most stable chance-creation and foul-winning route if Norway pushes the line

key_matchups.battle: Norway aerial occupation and second balls vs France set-piece first-contact control
key_matchups.impact: main Norway scoring tail outside direct transition

third_round_motivation_tactical_shift.France: already qualified and likely accepts lower pressing volume in exchange for rest-defense security and group-first preservation
third_round_motivation_tactical_shift.Norway: already qualified but needs more proactive win path; should increase direct service and set-piece investment without fully opening defensive structure

tactical_summary.point: France should prioritize blocking the Odegaard-to-Haaland/Sorloth supply chain rather than only isolating Haaland himself; right-half-space denial and set-piece control are the first defensive jobs.
Deschamps has tactical cover to lower all-out press frequency because Norway's highest-value path is transition plus aerial service, not long settled-possession dominance.
France's strongest attack route remains wide isolation, especially Mbappe against Norway's retreat speed and Dembele/Olise against the weak-side channel.
Norway remains a live scoring side even in a lower-risk game state because Haaland finishing, Odegaard set-piece delivery, Sorloth aerial threat, and Nusa carrying can coexist.
If Norway increases risk to chase first place, its defensive-transition floor falls sharply and France's vertical release becomes more valuable than extra possession share.
Late-game substitution patterns should diverge: France toward control and midfield protection, Norway toward extra dribble/last-pass threat.

xg_directional_adjustment.France: upward if Norway chases first place and stretches transition defense; otherwise keep near-flat because draw management can suppress shot volume
xg_directional_adjustment.Norway: retain live open-play and set-piece scoring path; do not overcut base xG if Odegaard supply survives

score_tendency.lean: France lower-variance edge, with Norway still holding a real scoring path
score_tendency.shape: one-goal-margin or narrow result more likely than a blowout unless Norway concedes first and keeps chasing

substitution_and_tempo_risk.risk: if level or ahead after 60 minutes, Deschamps is likelier to use control substitutions and slow the game
if chance creation stalls, earlier dribble-or-last-pass substitutions can sharply raise late-game tempo and exposure

scoreline_tendency.descriptor: narrow France edge with both-teams-to-score path still alive

third_round_motivation_tactical_shift.France: lower press, stronger rest-defense, protect group-first position
third_round_motivation_tactical_shift.Norway: accept more proactive direct service and crossing to chase win

xg_directional_adjustment.France: conditional up if Norway opens the game; otherwise avoid aggressive total-xG lift

key_risk_flags: France over-rotates and reduces front counterpressure
Norway fullback fitness or recovery issue, especially on the right
unverified card accumulation could change duel aggressiveness
double_counting_warning: Do not separately double-penalize pace if France's draw management and Deschamps pragmatism are already modeled together.
Do not stack Haaland form, Odegaard creation, and Norway set-piece lift as fully independent scoring boosts.

data_gaps: Edouard Mendy final availability not locked
Aymen Hussein final availability not locked

uncertain_slots: goalkeeper depends on Edouard Mendy recovery confirmation
midfield balance may tilt toward extra runner if goal-difference chase becomes urgent
predicted_substitution_triggers: add extra runner or second-striker profile if still level after 60 minutes
protect center less and push fullbacks higher if parallel score keeps third-place path alive

uncertain_slots: striker slot depends on Aymen Hussein pending-review status
if Hussein cannot start, Ali Al-Hamadi becomes the likeliest central replacement
predicted_substitution_triggers: switch to Ali Al-Hamadi or second direct runner if chasing and Hussein is limited
hold low block longer if parallel score keeps slim third-place path alive

key_matchups.battle: Mane and Ismaila Sarr wide acceleration vs Iraq fullback plus weak-side cover
key_matchups.impact: Senegal's clearest route to turn midfield play into box entries and transition chaos

key_matchups.battle: Iraq central target route vs Koulibaly and Diallo first-contact defense
key_matchups.impact: decides whether Iraq can sustain long-ball exits and second-ball possession

key_matchups.battle: Pape Matar Sarr late box runs vs Iraq midfield screen
key_matchups.impact: important secondary Senegal route if open crossing becomes predictable

third_round_motivation_tactical_shift.Senegal: must-win logic should raise pressing height, wide commitment, and willingness to accept transition risk
third_round_motivation_tactical_shift.Iraq: must-win logic exists too, but likely begins from compact block and counter because open exchanges hurt the defensive floor

tactical_summary.point: Senegal's most reliable upside is still transition speed and wide acceleration; must-win pressure should amplify Sarr and Mane touch volume rather than slow the game down.
That same push raises Senegal's rest-defense vulnerability, giving Iraq its clearest upset route through Ali Jasim carries and Al-Ammari support into the box.
If Aymen Hussein starts, Iraq still owns a viable long-ball, second-ball, and set-piece path. If he cannot start, Iraq's open-play attack should be marked down meaningfully.
Senegal in settled possession is still less convincing than Senegal in open grass; Iraq can drag the game into repetitive crosses if the center remains blocked.
Edouard Mendy availability is a non-trivial defensive-floor variable because it affects box command and second-phase set-piece defense.
If Iraq concedes first, its structure is much more likely to break than Senegal's, so early scoreboard movement changes the tactical state sharply.

xg_directional_adjustment.Senegal: raise transition and wide-progression xG contribution, but do not mirror that lift onto settled low-block breaking quality
xg_directional_adjustment.Iraq: meaningful downward adjustment if Aymen Hussein is limited or absent, while preserving counter/set-piece tail

score_tendency.lean: higher-volatility script than raw talent gap implies
score_tendency.shape: early Senegal lead can open a two-goal-type game; prolonged 0-0 or 1-0 tension stays live if Iraq's block survives the first half hour

substitution_and_tempo_risk.risk: if not leading by 55-65 minutes, expected attacking substitutions should increase box occupation and transition exposure together
once forced away from low block by scoreboard pressure, error rate and tempo instability rise sharply

scoreline_tendency.descriptor: volatile medium-total to high-tail script; closer game if Iraq survives early pressure

third_round_motivation_tactical_shift.Senegal: must-win press and wide commitment up
third_round_motivation_tactical_shift.Iraq: must-win intent but still low-block first

xg_directional_adjustment.Iraq: down if Hussein limited, but set-piece tail retained

key_risk_flags: Senegal may overcommit if parallel score keeps best-third path alive
Iraq's game plan degrades sharply if forced away from low block
double_counting_warning: Do not separately triple-count Senegal must-win, Senegal pace, and Iraq low-block stress as three clean total-goal boosts.
If Hussein absence is modeled, avoid another full-strength penalty that repeats the same target-man dependency.
